params.name: size
params.description: The maximum number of records per page.

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of records to skip before returning records. For example, with an offset of 20 and limit of 10, you will get records from 21 to 30.

params.name: priceListId
params.description: The price list ID to which the item belongs. If not provided, the default price list is used.

items.docs: Get a paginated list of active and scheduled prices. Active prices have start date greater than the current date and end date is in the future.
Create or update price of an item based on given `itemId` and `itemSku`

params.name: size
params.description: The maximum number of records per page.

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of records to skip before returning records. For example, with an offset of 20 and limit of 10, you will get records from 21 to 30.

params.name: priceListId
params.description: The price list ID to which the item belongs. If not provided, the default price list is used.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The item ID from which prices are retrieved.

params.name: status
params.description:
The status used to filter prices. The following statuses are available:
- `ACTIVE`: Displays current prices or regular prices. These are the prices that are actively displayed and applied to products on an ongoing basis.
- `SCHEDULED`: Displays prices that will be applied to products in the future. Retailers often use scheduled prices for marketing campaigns to attract customers, generate sales, clear inventory, or create a sense of urgency.
- `ALL`: Displays both `ACTIVE` and `SCHEDULED` prices.

items.docs: Get a paginated list of price details for the given `itemId` and `priceListId`. If `priceListId` isn't specified, prices are retrieved for the `itemId` that belongs to the default price list.

params.name: priceListId
params.description: The price list ID to which the item belongs. If not provided, the default price list is used.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The item ID of the price record that's being deleted.

items.docs: Delete price details by `itemId`. If `priceListId` isn't specified, price is deleted for the `itemId` that belongs to the default price list.

params.name: size
params.description: The maximum number of records per page.

params.name: priceListId
params.description: The price list ID to which the item belongs. If not provided, the default price list is used.

params.name: sku
params.description: Product SKU to get the price record for

params.name: status
params.description:
The status used to filter prices. The following statuses are available:
- `ACTIVE`: Displays current prices or regular prices. These are the prices that are actively displayed and applied to products on an ongoing basis.
- `SCHEDULED`: Displays prices that will be applied to products in the future. Retailers often use scheduled prices for marketing campaigns to attract customers, generate sales, clear inventory, or create a sense of urgency.
- `ALL`: Displays both `ACTIVE` and `SCHEDULED` prices.

items.docs: Get price details for the `sku` and `priceListId`. If `priceListId` isn't specified, price is retrieved for the `sku` that belongs to the default price list.

params.name: priceListId
params.description: The price list ID to which the item belongs. If not provided, the default price list is used.

params.name: sku
params.description: The product SKU of the price details that are being deleted.
